---
title: "Summary"
author: "Laurie Platt"
date: last-modified
date-format: "[Last updated ] DD MMMM, YYYY"
format:
  html:
    code-tools: true
    self-contained: false
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 5
    toc-expand: 2
    number-sections: true
    fig-cap-location: top
    code-links:
      - text: scc-pi/traffic-collisions
        icon: github
        href: https://github.com/scc-pi/traffic-collisions
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

The Council has statutory duties under the Road Traffic Act 1988 and broader public health responsibilities to improve road safety and reduce the number of Killed or Seriously Injured (KSI) traffic collisions.  

In 2024 there were 255 KSIs. 40% of these were children and young people, forty-five under 16s and fifty-nine 16-to-24-year-olds. Reducing these numbers is a priority (Council Plan priorities Children & Young People, and City on the Move).   

Problem statement:  

> “Given imperfect data and limited budgets, where should we intervene next to prevent severe harm?” 

Proposal summary:  

1. Historic collision frequency alone does not capture future risk.  
1. Use additional publicly available data, including road network, traffic flow, and socio-economic indicators.  
1. Severity‑weightings are a policy not a statistical decision.  
1. Transparency and explainability prioritised over “black box” AI models.  
1. Follow existing good practice e.g. TfL.  

```{mermaid}
%%| label: gap-analysis
%%| fig-cap: "Figure 1: Gap Analysis - Current vs Future Road Safety Decision Journey"
%%| echo: true
%%| code-fold: hide

flowchart TD

    %% Current State
    subgraph CS[<b>Current State</b>]
        C1[Historic collision counts]
        C2[Hotspot ranking]
        C3[Officer judgement]
        C4[Scheme selection]

        C1 --> C2 --> C3 --> C4
    end

    %% Future State
    subgraph FS[<b>Future State</b>]
        F1[Integrated risk indicators]
        F2[Predictive risk scoring]
        F3[Officer judgement + evidence]
        F4[Proactive intervention]

        F1 --> F2 --> F3 --> F4
    end

    %% Alignment (optional visual linkage)
    C1 -.-> F1
    C2 -.-> F2
    C3 -.-> F3
    C4 -.-> F4
```

People:  

- Laurie Platt (Data Scientist)  
- Nicola Mchugh (Corporate Performance & Insight Manager)  
- Tracy Hendry (Road Safety Manager)  
- Ashley Carnall (Road Safety Auditor)

Timeline (Laurie’s Multiverse coursework deadlines):  

- June 2026 - Proposal ([Business transformation proposal](https://sccextranet.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/DataScience/Shared%20Documents/Public/RoadSafety/Multiverse/Module05-ProjectMgt/ADF_M5_Project_LauriePlatt.docx?d=w30acc46970554540b358c73b32d59556&csf=1&web=1&e=ma6JQc))  
- Feb 2027 - Risk model - Live (Capstone project)  

Next Steps: 

- Further research on best practice (e.g. TfL) and planning (as part of current Multiverse coursework).  
- Meeting with Tracy, Ash, & Nicola – sponsorship, proposal sign off, ways of working etc.  

Detailed [project plan](https://github.com/orgs/scc-pi/projects/7/views/1).
